top_warnings.title: `tests/test_ai_config.py` is 523 lines — slightly above the 500-line warning threshold.

open_items.title: `tests/test_ai_config.py` is 523 lines — slightly above the 500-line warning threshold.
`PyYAML` is a runtime dependency used by `ai_config.py` but is not listed in
`tests/test_ai_dispatch.py` is 588 lines — above the 500-line soft warning.
`ProviderCallAdapter` is a Protocol with no concrete implementation yet.
`datetime.datetime.utcnow()` produces deprecation warnings in Python 3.12.
test_ai_dispatch.py is 588 lines — above 500-line warning
datetime.utcnow() deprecation in ai_dispatch.py
ProviderCallAdapter has no concrete HTTP implementation
lead_scoring_engine.py is 651 lines with 4 functions above 40-line warning
TokenAuthenticator uses static token map — needs JWT/session integration for production
Redis pub/sub adapter not implemented — single-instance broadcasting only
websockets package installed but unused — Starlette built-in used instead
`lead_scoring_engine.py` is already at 652 lines (exceeds 500-line warning threshold). Adding campaign enrollment callbacks to scoring flow should be done via event hooks, not by extending the file.
Double opt-in flow is recommended for maximum GDPR compliance but adds complexity. Implementation team should decide on single vs double opt-in before coding.

Email provider integration (SMTP/SendGrid/SES) is out of scope for this architecture review but must be decided before implementation. Recommend an abstract `EmailProvider` interface with pluggable backends.
`persistence.py` at 474 lines — approaching 500-line warn threshold. Consider splitting row mappers into a separate module if more tables are added.
`UNSUBSCRIBE_SECRET` in `consent_manager.py` is hardcoded — should be loaded from environment variable for production deployment.
Pre-existing `datetime.utcnow()` deprecation warnings in `test_ai_dispatch.py` (30 warnings) — not in PR-6 scope but should be addressed.
Double opt-in deferred per plan (OI-054) — single opt-in implemented for MVP as specified.
UNSUBSCRIBE_SECRET hardcoded in consent_manager.py — needs env var for production
test_campaign_integration.py is 595 lines — exceeds 500-line warning threshold

recent_closures.title: No email sent without explicit user consent record
recent_closures.closed_reason: evidence: Single send path in campaign_service.py:send_campaign_email. verify_consent() is first call (line 133). Returns no_consent if false. No alternative send paths exist.

recent_closures.title: Unsubscribe mechanism functional in all campaign templates
recent_closures.closed_reason: evidence: HMAC-signed unsubscribe tokens in consent_manager.py. GET/POST /unsubscribe/{token} endpoints in campaigns.py:144-178. Idempotent — checks if already unsubscribed. CampaignTemplate.__post_init__ enforces mandatory unsubscribe_url.

recent_closures.title: No function exceeds 40 lines (warning) or 70 lines (blocker)
recent_closures.closed_reason: evidence: AST analysis shows max function is chi_squared_significance at 43 lines (warn only). No function exceeds 70-line blocker.

recent_closures.title: No Python file exceeds 500 lines (warning) or 800 lines (blocker)
recent_closures.closed_reason: evidence: Max source file is persistence.py at 474 lines (under 500 warn). All other PR-6 source files under 310 lines.

recent_closures.title: No database persistence layer exists — consent records REQUIRE durable storage for GDPR compliance. Must be resolved before PR-6 implementation starts.
recent_closures.closed_reason: evidence: SQLite persistence via aiosqlite in persistence.py. DatabaseManager initialized in main.py lifespan, schema auto-created. consent_records, campaigns, campaign_enrollments, ab_test_configs, ab_test_assignments tables. test_consent_survives_db_reconnect confirms durability.
